GT LEARN #621 ⏰ Task Scheduler MEDIUM Heap Greedy Queue
tasks= n= exp: 8

📝 Problem Statement

CPU ke paas ek list of tasks (A-Z chars) hai. Har task 1 unit time leta hai. Same task ke do executions ke beech mein at least n units ka cooldown gap hona chahiye. Idle slots allowed hain. Find minimum total time to finish all tasks.

🧠 Solving Approach

Greedy: har time-step pe most-frequent available task ko run karo. Use a max-heap for active tasks + a cooldown queue for tasks waiting for their cooldown to expire.

🎯 Core Concepts

Max-Heap: Always pick task with highest remaining count. Python's heapq is min-heap, so push -count.
Cooldown Queue: After running a task, push it to a queue with (time + n) as the time when it becomes available again.
Time Tick Loop: Each iteration = 1 time unit. Increment time, then process heap pop + queue release.
⏱️ Time: O(T·log 26) 💾 Space: O(26)

🧪 Test Cases (click to load)

basicA,B equal — needs idle slots→ 8
tasks=["A","A","A","B","B","B"]n=2
no-idleEnough variety — no idle→ 6
tasks=["A","C","A","B","D","B"]n=1
zero-cooln=0 — no cooldown needed→ 6
tasks=["A","A","A","B","B","B"]n=0
large-nBig cooldown — many idles→ 10
tasks=["A","A","A","B","B","B"]n=3
dominantA dominates — fillers help→ 16
tasks=["A","A","A","A","A","A","B","C","D","E","F","G"]n=2
—
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization
0/0 —
←→R 100%
